title: Russia and North Korea connect automobile bridge spans over Tumannaya River in historic infrastructure milestone
summary: Russia and North Korea completed the span connection of the first road bridge between the two countries over the Tumannaya River, with opening scheduled for June 2026, deepening bilateral infrastructure ties.
contents:
## Russia-DPRK Road Bridge Achieves Key Construction Milestone

### Development
On April 21, 2026, construction crews connected the superstructure spans of the new automobile bridge over the Tumannaya River at the Russia-DPRK border, marking a historic milestone in bilateral relations.

### Project Details
- Location: Khasan settlement (Primorsky Territory, Russia) to DPRK border
- This represents the first direct road connection between Russia and North Korea
- Previously only railway service connected the two nations
- The bridge opening target: June 2026

### Diplomatic Context
- Russian Transport Ministry officials and senior delegates participated via video link
- The construction agreement originated from President Putin's June 2024 visit to Pyongyang
- Russian Interior Minister Kolokoltsev simultaneously visited Pyongyang for security cooperation discussions
- Plans for Russian-Korean military cooperation 2027-2031 announced

### Strategic Significance
- The bridge will facilitate increased trade and movement between Russia and North Korea
- Construction proceeds despite international sanctions on North Korea
- The project raises concerns about potential sanctions evasion through enhanced logistics
- Vladivostok customs authorities already report significant increases in cross-border passenger traffic

### Source Attribution
Russian Ministry of Transport, TASS/Ruptly reporting, DPRK state media.
